claim: N348I mutations decrease resistance to zidovudine (AZT).

result.claim: N348I mutations decrease resistance to zidovudine (AZT).
result.reasoning: The claim states that N348I mutations decrease resistance to zidovudine (AZT). However, evidence from paper ID 15319019, titled "N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance," directly refutes this. Proposition 1 from this paper explicitly states, "N348I decreased zidovudine susceptibility 2- to 4-fold." The abstract of the same paper further confirms this finding: "N348I decreased zidovudine susceptibility 2- to 4-fold in the context of wildtype HIV-1 or when combined with TAMs." Decreased susceptibility to a drug indicates an *increase* in resistance, not a decrease. Therefore, the N348I mutation confers increased resistance to zidovudine.

evidence.text: In KRAS mutant tumors, PIK3CA mutation restores Cyclin D1 expression and G1/S cell cycle progression.
evidence.source: PIK3CA mutation uncouples tumor growth and Cyclin D1 regulation from MEK/ERK and mutant KRAS signaling

evidence.text: The odds ratio for the selection of N348I in patients treated with zidovudine and nevirapine was 2.62.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: S10 mutations were associated with poor outcome in metastatic breast cancers.
evidence.source: Genomic characterization of metastatic breast cancers

evidence.text: The 1100delC mutation was present in 0.4% of the controls.
evidence.source: Linkage Disequilibrium Mapping of       CHEK2: Common Variation and Breast Cancer Risk

evidence.text: Re-expression of mutant PIK3CA reinstates the resistance to MEK inhibition.
evidence.source: PIK3CA mutation uncouples tumor growth and Cyclin D1 regulation from MEK/ERK and mutant KRAS signaling

evidence.text: N348I decreased susceptibility to efavirenz 2.5-fold.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: N348I was highly associated with thymidine analogue mutations M41L and T215Y/F with a p-value less than 0.001.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Mice with Kras and Lkb1 mutations had primary resistance to this combination therapy.
evidence.source: A murine lung cancer co-clinical trial identifies genetic modifiers of therapeutic response

evidence.text: The 1100delC mutation occurred in 0.4% of controls in the Swedish population.
evidence.source: Linkage Disequilibrium Mapping of       CHEK2: Common Variation and Breast Cancer Risk

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 inhibited the proliferation of HER2-amplified BT474 cells exogenously expressing mutations that render them resistant to trastuzumab.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: S13 mutations were associated with poor outcome in metastatic breast cancers.
evidence.source: Genomic characterization of metastatic breast cancers

evidence.text: N348I was associated with non-nucleoside RTI resistance mutations K103N and Y181C/I with a p-value less than 0.001.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: NT5C2 mutant proteins conferred resistance to chemotherapy with 6-thioguanine when expressed in ALL lymphoblasts.
evidence.source: Activating mutations in the NT5C2 nucleotidase gene drive chemotherapy resistance in relapsed ALL
